setting.note: Verghese's Addis Ababa is a highland capital of eucalyptus, corrugated iron and mission medicine, seen from a hospital where twin brothers are born and grow up. The map holds the old town around Piazza, the cathedral and the emperor's palace, and follows the city through the fall of Haile Selassie and the rise of the Derg before the story leaves Ethiopia for exile abroad.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Abraham Verghese, first published 2009. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: Cutting for Stone, Abraham Verghese (2009). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The red earth and rust-iron roofs of highland Addis Ababa. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
